topic: c2 verification — ISS-2209 E-PRED-C inversion sentence + ISS-2210 TLDR snapshot-date framing both close cleanly; no new holes
change_summary: Decider commit fc9999f (2026-05-27T14:43Z) applied two patches to data/sections.json p4-424: (1) ISS-2209 inserted the E-PRED-C inversion sentence into the opening paragraph immediately after the Tier 1/2/3 clause — character-for-character identical to the SEC-8b.3 L3a version (423 chars, including the <a href="#win014"> cross-reference and onclick tab-switch); (2) ISS-2210 changed the TLDR opening from 'The dome registers four' to 'The dome’s 2026-03-12 snapshot registered four', matching the body framing. docs/index.html rebuilt in the same commit. 3692/3692 tests pass per commit message.
our_argument_summary: SEC-4.2.4 examines the four E-PRED sub-tests (A peak-tracks-geometry, B Hartland-beats-Ebro, C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, D non-path stations below noise floor) registered in the dome's 2026-03-12 snapshot for the August 2026 eclipse, finds three non-discriminating + one (E-PRED-C, gravity null) that favors the standard ionospheric model, and now (post-patches) anchors the enumeration to the snapshot date AND surfaces the E-PRED-C inversion — the 2026-05-07 live page replaces the 0.0 µGal prediction with PRED-TIER-3's positive 10–13 µGal pulse for the same instrument class at the same eclipse — directly to readers of the main eclipse discussion section.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review — N/A at WIN granularity. Body still cites monitor.py hardcoded ecm_eclipse literals and fsf_derivation.py confirmation; the new opening paragraph and TLDR are descriptive prose and do not change the code-analysis substrate documented in c1.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: E-PRED-A (peak tracks geometry), E-PRED-B (Hartland>Ebro), E-PRED-D (non-path stations below noise floor) all reduce to standard Chapman-Sq mechanism predictions on a spherical Earth. Only E-PRED-C makes a discriminating prediction — and the body shows it favors the standard model. The new inversion sentence strengthens this by surfacing that the dome itself has now superseded E-PRED-C with the OPPOSITE-sign Tier-3 prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The four E-PRED sub-tests are registered for an August 12, 2026 eclipse that has not yet occurred. Verified directly in raw-text/03-predictions.txt: EXTRACTED 2026-03-12, lines 37-40 enumerate E-PRED-A/B/C/D, line 18 ties them to the August 12 2026 eclipse. The post-snapshot Tier 1/2/3 restructuring (per EXP-292 live-page fetch) does not retroactively withdraw the 2026-03-12 commitments.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: FSF = H(r)/r reduces to tan(latitude)/tan(ref_lat) by the dome's own r×tan(lat)=H(r) placement equation — any firmament height function cancels algebraically (body Section 4.2.4 E-PRED-B paragraph, station-by-station verification). The numerical predictions are tan(latitude) restated; the new inversion sentence does not introduce any new dome-geometry derivation claim.

relevant_files: data/sections.json (part8b html, L3a for cross-page parity comparison)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section is descriptive prose plus per-sub-test analysis — no calculation pipeline introduced by the patches. The body still cites monitor.py ecm_eclipse hardcoded literal and fsf_derivation.py.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Patches are descriptive prose only. No literal values introduced or modified.
data_sources_used: raw-text/03-predictions.txt EXTRACTED 2026-03-12 (verified directly: file present, EXTRACTED date matches, E-PRED-A/B/C/D labels at lines 37-40 match enumeration verbatim, August 12 2026 eclipse window at line 18)
EXP-292 verification_performed snapshot of 2026-05-07 live-page state (re-used; not independently re-fetched in this review)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None new. The rhetorical 'intellectually dishonest' framing carried over from prior text. New inversion sentence is factual and falsifiable: a defender who can show that PRED-TIER-3 was NOT registered for SG gravimeters at the August 2026 eclipse, or that the 10-13 µGal range is wrong, can directly attack it.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. ISS-2209 and ISS-2210 replace patterns matched verbatim against the suggested-patches file. The integrated SEC-4.2.4 inversion sentence is character-for-character identical to the SEC-8b.3 L3a sentence (423 chars, both confirmed). The TLDR change matches the patch file ('The dome registers four' → 'The dome’s 2026-03-12 snapshot registered four').

holes_found.description: Cross-reference precision: the new inversion sentence sends readers to WIN-014 'for the resulting self-contradiction analysis,' but WIN-014's primary verdict is 'Refuted by Data' driven by the 2009 China SG-network empirical null. WIN-014 detail_evidence does discuss the PRED-TIER-3 / κ-coupling self-contradiction (verified: phrase 'PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation, because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target' present in detail_evidence, and the explicit comment that the 'self-contradiction with PRED-TIER-3 framing — true but secondary — now lives in the cluster's vise'). So WIN-014 IS a valid cross-reference target, but the primary self-contradiction analysis now lives in the κ-cluster section (p6-614-kappa-cluster), with WIN-014 carrying it as secondary framing. A reader who follows the WIN-014 link expecting the inversion analysis will find the empirical refutation first and the inversion argument folded into a κ-cluster cross-reference. Mirror-parity with SEC-8b.3 — which uses the same WIN-014 cite — is preserved, so this is a design choice (consistent with the response-to-critics section) rather than an error. A dome defender attacking the sentence would attack the inversion claim itself, not this citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add a secondary cross-reference to p6-614-kappa-cluster (e.g., 'see WIN-014 and the κ-coupling cluster for the resulting self-contradiction analysis') so readers find the structural argument directly. Low priority — SEC-8b.3 uses the same citation pattern, parity is intentional, and WIN-014 does carry enough cross-reference traffic to land readers in the right place.

stronger_arguments.description: Surface the inversion in the TLDR itself. Current TLDR (319 chars) still characterizes E-PRED-C as 'gravity null' that 'favors the standard ionospheric model.' A skim-reader who reads only the TLDR misses the strongest argument that now lives in the body (the dome's own active protocol has the OPPOSITE-sign prediction). A one-clause addition — e.g., '…actually favors the standard model (and has since been inverted in the dome's own 2026-05-07 protocol).' — would carry the strongest framing to skim-readers without blowing the TLDR length budget. Tradeoff: TLDR present-tense framing was just stabilized; another patch might re-destabilize it. Not pursued as a hole because the body now carries the inversion explicitly and a defender cannot legitimately claim hidden framing.
sources: data/sections.json:p4-424 opening paragraph inversion sentence (the body framing being mirrored upward)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome team's 2026-05-07 live-page restructuring DID add a new public protocol with its own timestamps (the PRED-TIER-1/2/3 Tier protocol per EXP-292 verification_performed). They did not hide the change — git history preserves the restructuring, and a reader visiting the live dome page sees the new Tier protocol with PRED-TIER-3 explicitly described (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:1-22 establishes the cryptographic timestamping framing the dome itself uses, and EXP-292 captured the post-restructure live-page state). A charitable defender can argue 'model iteration as the August 2026 eclipse approached and new data informed the prediction structure.' The new inversion sentence in SEC-4.2.4 acknowledges the restructuring exists ('the 2026-05-07 live page replaces it…') rather than alleging deletion or concealment — this is fair characterization.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Model iteration without explicit WITHDRAWN notation is incompatible with the dome's OWN epistemic framing of cryptographic pre-registration as 'highest evidential weight.' Verified directly in raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 12: 'PROSPECTIVE: Timestamped before confirming data (Highest evidential weight — true advance prediction).' The entire claim to higher evidential weight rests on the predictions being immutable once timestamped. Silently restructuring (and, for E-PRED-C specifically, INVERTING from 0.0 µGal to +10-13 µGal — same instrument class, same eclipse, opposite sign) those predictions undermines the very pre-registration claim that distinguishes them from postdictions. SEC-4.2.4 now correctly surfaces this — 'ignoring registered predictions because they were quietly relabeled would be intellectually dishonest' — and the new inversion sentence makes the case empirically: a defender holding the dome to the 2026-03-12 enumeration is being fair on the dome's OWN cryptographic-timestamping terms. The patches close the c1 cross-page parity gap and the c1 TLDR present-tense framing gap; neither defender argument from c1 kernel-of-truth ('iteration not relabeling') survives the explicit OPPOSITE-sign inversion the new sentence documents.

deeper_analysis: Both patches landed cleanly. ISS-2209: the 423-char inversion sentence is character-for-character identical between SEC-4.2.4 (p4-424 opening paragraph) and SEC-8b.3 L3a (p8b-3-eclipse-layers, the third L3a sub-paragraph) — verified by direct byte comparison via node script. Cross-page parity restored. ISS-2210: the TLDR opening changed from present-tense 'The dome registers four' to snapshot-anchored 'The dome’s 2026-03-12 snapshot registered four', exactly matching the c1 recommendation. The TLDR still uses scare-quoted 'model-discriminating' and present-tense 'favors the standard ionospheric model' — both are stylistic choices that the c1 review explicitly noted as acceptable (scare quotes carry the dome's framing; 'favors' is a verdict-style claim). No new factual errors introduced. The single new finding (minor): the WIN-014 cross-reference is intentionally mirrored from SEC-8b.3 for parity, but WIN-014's primary thrust is empirical refutation, with the inversion analysis now living in p6-614-kappa-cluster (per WIN-014 detail_evidence opening paragraph). This is a design choice consistent with the prior parity, not an error. A defender attacking the new sentence would attack its factual content (E-PRED-C inversion, PRED-TIER-3 10-13 µGal, same instrument class), not the citation — all three are verified against raw-text/03-predictions.txt, EXP-292 verification_performed, and WIN-014 detail_evidence. Recommend no_change. The decider should pop qid-409 and close.

summary_for_decider: PATCH VERIFICATION CLEAN. ISS-2209 (moderate, c1) closed: inversion sentence integrated verbatim, byte-identical to SEC-8b.3 L3a parity model. ISS-2210 (minor, c1) closed: TLDR snapshot-anchored, matches c1 recommendation. No critical/major holes. One new minor observation: WIN-014 cross-reference precision — the primary self-contradiction analysis now lives in p6-614-kappa-cluster with WIN-014 carrying it as secondary framing, but parity with SEC-8b.3 (same cite) is intentional and the citation does resolve. Stronger-argument note (not raised as hole): TLDR could surface the inversion to skim-readers (+~80 chars feasible). recommended_action=no_change. Pop qid-409 strict (queue_id match).
